titleStmt.title: Letter from George A. Lyons to the Honorary Secretary of Sinn Féin, 5 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: George A. Lyons

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from George A. Lyons, Honorary Secretary, B. Company, Third Battallion, Irish Volunteers, to the Honorary Secretary of Sinn Féin inquiring about the cost of hiring a room on 29 April for a 'smoker' or a recruitment event. George A. Lyons (d. 1950) fought in the Boland's Mill Garrison during Easter Week. He was imprisoned in Frongoch, Wandsworth and Lincoln. Prior to the rising Lyons had been a founding member of the Irish Republican Brotherhood and was an active participant in the War of Independence.

msIdentifier.collection: Allen Library, Br. Allen Collection, IE/AL/1916/7

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
B. Coy--- Third Battalion I.V. 144 Gt. Brunswick St. 5 4/16 A Chara, The above propose holding
a 'smoker ' on Sat. 29th inst.
and would like to know for
what figure they could have the
use of your room if then vacant Yours Fraternally,
Geo. A. Lyons
Hon. Sec.
The Hon. Sec.
Sinn Fein.
